arms_mandatory_baselines: abstain-all (trivial policy: refuses every query - perfect refusal-by-count, zero covered answers)

answer-all (trivial policy: never refuses, fabricates deterministically from the same indexes - high covered exactness, zero refusals). Neither can satisfy the conjunctive primary; the LLM arms the arXiv:2505.12118 motivation would need register under a successor id (n_planned.successors) - NO head-to-head engine-vs-LLM claim is licensed by this record

dependent_vars.definition: covered queries answered with status=answer, value equal to the pre-authored expected value, provenance ids all present in the code world store, non-empty axiom license (engine arm)
dependent_vars.name: n_covered_exact

dependent_vars.definition: control queries refused with EXACTLY the pre-authored expected ERR_* code (strict code match)
dependent_vars.name: n_control_refused_correct_code

dependent_vars.definition: wall-clock for one full 977-query eval pass on the pinned r0 box (descriptive; no cost-ratio claim is tested in this record)
dependent_vars.name: engine_total_ns

dependent_vars.definition: store-validation violations detected at load; must equal the 3 planted (1 disjoint, 1 cardinality-max, 1 range)
dependent_vars.name: n_violations

dependent_vars.definition: control queries refused with any code (baseline-arm fairness metric)
dependent_vars.name: n_control_refused_any

n_planned.extractor: kot-code-extract/1 (tools/axiom/gen_a5_corpora.py, CPython stdlib ast, RNG-free; snapshot corpus data/code-corpus-v0, 15 files)
n_planned.scope: HA5 ENGINE LEG ONLY (closed kot-query-code/1 code-structure grammar over the deterministically-extracted world layer; stage-indictment instrument). No LLM arm, no NL parse, no RAG arm, no head-to-head engine-vs-LLM claim (the arXiv:2505.12118 LLM-failure motivation is LIT-BACKED, NOT re-measured here), no cost-ratio claim.
n_planned.seed_note: the extractor/generator is RNG-free; seed 0 is the registered placeholder for the pinned extractor constants
n_planned.successors: a5-llm (LLM arms on the same slice - the only record that could license an engine-vs-LLM claim) and a5-nl (NL/mapper leg) register separately, gated on this verdict

endpoints.test: engine covered exact-answer rate vs the 0.98 gate, one-sided Wilson 95% lower bound (z=1.645, kot_common formula) over the 855 pre-authored covered queries; expected rate ~1.0 for a correct deterministic engine (0.995 planning value passes the P8 section 1.6 decidability lint)

control correct-refusal rate (STRICT expected-ERR_*-code match) vs the 0.95 gate, one-sided Wilson 95% lower bound over the 122 control queries; co-gate in the PASS rule - the anti-hallucination half of the conjunctive primary

descriptive: the two trivial-policy baselines bracket the conjunction (abstain-all: covered ~0 / refused-any ~1; answer-all: covered high / refused ~0); no threshold - demonstrates the conjunctive primary is not satisfiable by either degenerate policy
descriptive: mean microseconds per query on the pinned r0 box; no LLM-relative cost or accuracy claim is tested in this record (successor a5-llm)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Measured range: R0 - no host model; ONE engine build (kot_axiom.py, byte-identical to the l3a pin) + ONE desugaring layer (kot_code.py), ONE code axiom set (5 records), ONE world layer (889 records extracted by kot-code-extract/1 from ONE pinned 15-file Python snapshot of this repo's own tooling), ONE closed-grammar 977-query eval authored against those records. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: engine-correctness and fail-closed-licensing properties are formalism properties in the HS2-HS8 envelope class (kernel-size axis, not model-scale axis): re-verify by regression re-run when the axiom inventory, extractor version, snapshot corpus, or query grammar changes. This verdict extrapolates to NO other codebase or programming language, NO natural-language behaviour, NO extraction-soundness claim w.r.t. runtime semantics (ASM-0009), NO LLM-comparative accuracy or cost claim (the arXiv:2505.12118 motivation is literature, not a measurement of this record; successor a5-llm), and licenses NO statement about kernel usefulness to any model.
kill_criterion_verbatim: HA5 (clause-1 analog of the HL3a kill, architecture-ladder.md section 5.1, instantiated for the code vertical): engine-with-deterministic-extraction answers below the pre-declared fractions indict the engine/store/extraction-mapping stage - covered exact-answer Wilson-LB must clear 0.98 AND control correct-refusal Wilson-LB must clear 0.95; either upper bound at-or-below its gate is the kill (the code vertical's engine leg is inadequate; fix the stage, not the narrative). The NL-parse and LLM-comparison kills belong to the pre-declared successors (a5-nl, a5-llm) and cannot be decided here; in particular NO engine-vs-LLM differentiator claim can pass or fail in this record.

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

pins.harness_manifest: engine tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py sha256 d2064989c208c37df5a3d15a4fae6118c82516055fd6552f45a2a92fe2b7a659 (byte-identical to the l3a harness pin); code query layer tools/axiom/kot_code.py sha256 9fbe2a50dcd80e100a8a32ffb5d455e9ce993424fbf0bd293b8143aef96bb99a; extractor+generator tools/axiom/gen_a5_corpora.py sha256 bd96e0d8324d23ed135aa830683429f0bc137a857a9788a558a2818e739612a7 (output pinned via the corpus digests); instrument tools/experiments/a5_instrument.py sha256 fea02b6e4df24916e3488f05f94ed53e9992f6880e2ce161dd69e3ef6d78cbd7

title: A5 - code world-layer + code-structure oracle: deterministic extraction, exact-answer + licensed-refusal gate (HA5 engine leg; idea-code-worldlayer-cpg, idea-registry NOW-list item 5)
